The Target / what we are here to win

Three commercial keywords. One local market.

These are patient searches with buying intent in Kelowna. We already appear for all three. The job is to move from ranked to Top 3, and from impressions to clicks.

Keyword
kelowna dentist
VOL 480 KD 52% COMMERCIAL
Keyword
dentist kelowna
VOL 1.3K KD 56% COMMERCIAL
Keyword
dentist in kelowna
VOL 170 KD 56% COMMERCIAL

Source: SEMrush keyword data, 01 Jul 2026

The Foundation / what is already working

You are not starting from zero.

Before the fixes, it helps to name the strengths. Discover Dental already has a real base to build on, which is why moving to the Top 3 is realistic rather than wishful.

✓Ranked for all three keywords

The site already appears for every target term. The visibility is there to build on.

✓265,000+ impressions in 3 months

Google is actively surfacing the site across a wide range of patient queries.

✓Strong on desktop

Performance 87 and an SEO score of 92. The technical core is already sound.

✓40 blog posts already ranking

Real patient questions, already answered and already linking back to the homepage.

✓AI is already crawling

Thirteen pages have been discovered by AI. The door to citation is already open.

✓Level with a page-1 rival

Authority Score 10, the same as Okanagan Dentistry. The gap to page one is closeable.

The Problem / why this audit exists

Impressions are landing. Clicks are not.

SEO has been running, and Google is showing the site. Over the last three months the site pulled more than a quarter of a million impressions. Almost none of them turned into a visit. That gap is the whole opportunity, and it is fixable.

Impressions · 3 months
0
Google is already surfacing the site across queries
Overall click rate
0
Visibility exists. The click is being left on the table
Where the keywords sit
10-15
Ranked, but below the fold where patients never scroll

Four levers close this gap. Technical raises the ceiling. Off-page wins the tie. On-page earns the click. Content compounds the authority. The rest of this audit walks each one, then connects them.

Technical SEO / 01 · page experience

The site is fast on desktop and slow where it counts.

Patients search on phones. On mobile the main content takes 6.8 seconds to appear and the layout shifts while it loads. Both are well outside Google's healthy range, and both are fixable behind the scenes.

Mobile performance
0
▲ Needs work
Desktop performance
0
● Healthy
Mobile LCP
0
▲ Target under 2.5s
Mobile CLS
0
▲ Target under 0.1

Both page-1 competitors load faster than we do

Speed is a tiebreaker. When trust and content are close, the faster site wins the position. Right now the two competitors ranking above us are both quicker on mobile.

▲ MOBILE 50 · LCP 6.8s ✓ DESKTOP 87 · LCP 1.2s

＋ Tap to zoom

Mobile · Performance 64

Desktop · Performance 75
The fix. Clean up what is slowing the page behind the scenes, stop the layout jumping on phones, and load images and fonts in the right order. The patient should not have to wait after they tap.
Technical SEO / 02 · AI readability

AI is crawling the site and citing none of it.

ChatGPT, Gemini, and Google's AI Overviews read a page the way a screen reader does. When the layout shifts or links are unlabelled, they skip or only partly read it. That is one likely reason the site is crawled by AI yet never cited.

Agentic browsing score
1 / 3
▲ Hard for agents to read
Pages crawled by AI
0
The content is being discovered
Citation rate · AI Overviews & Gemini
0
Discovered, but never quoted back
The fix. The same mobile and stability work above, plus labelling every clickable element in the background so an agent can identify it. Readable structure is what earns the citation.
Technical SEO / 03 · structured data

The schema tells Google what the site is, not who it is.

The site runs basic business schema only. The layer above it is missing: the part that tells Google and AI which entity this practice is, what it specialises in, and how it fits the wider knowledge graph. Three specific gaps.

01Entity schema is missing▲ GAP+

Traditional schema names the business. It does not tell an engine what the practice knows, what it is credentialed in, or what it belongs to. The attributes that do that are absent.

sameAsknowsAbouthasCredentialmemberOfalumniOfawardmainEntityOfPage

Adding these is like handing Google and the AI engines a machine-readable identity card instead of making them scrape the whole site to guess who you are.

＋ Zoom
Homepage · missing entity attributes
＋ Zoom
Service page · schema missing
Homepage · how it should be ↗ Service page · how it should be ↗
02The blog author is not real▲ GAP+

Blog posts show no author on the page, and in the schema the author is a placeholder first name, heet, with no photo and no credentials. Google is shifting from understanding websites to understanding entities: people, organisations, places. A real, detailed Person schema marks the dentist as a trusted entity. That supports E-E-A-T and makes the site more likely to be cited in both search and AI.

＋ Zoom
Blog post · no author on page
＋ Zoom
Schema · author = "heet" placeholder
How it should be ↗ Info needed from client ↗
03Homepage FAQ schema is thin◐ PARTIAL+

The homepage FAQ schema carries only two questions. Google has said FAQ markup rarely earns a rich result now, so this looks minor. It is not. FAQ structure still matters for AI engines and for Google's own understanding of the page. Filling it out is covering a corner most competitors leave open.

＋ Zoom
Homepage · only two FAQ entries
Technical SEO / 04 · indexing

Ten blog posts Google crawled and chose not to show.

Google has visited these pages and decided not to index them, which usually means the content reads generic or the page has no internal support. Each one is a missed chance to rank for a specific patient question, the kind that brings in someone close to booking.

◐ SET 1Likely needs internal links

Strong existing pages should reference these and pass authority their way.

/digital-x-rays-for-dental-implants-and-orthodontics /how-long-do-veneers-last /are-cerec-restorations-durable /warning-signs-of-oral-cancer /are-partial-dentures-right-for-you
▲ SET 2Likely needs content rework

The content itself needs to be more specific and useful before Google shows it.

/dental-first-aid-kit-for-parents /talk-to-your-dentist /understanding-baby-teeth-dental-emergencies /dental-bonding-candidate-signs /common-dental-problems-in-children
Sitemap. A minor but real fix. The sitemap is the roadmap Google follows. It simply needs to be resubmitted in Search Console so new and updated pages are found faster.
＋ Zoom
Technical SEO / what it earns

Where the technical work leads.

Do
Core Web Vitals · Schema · Sitemap · Indexing
Then
Google and AI understand the site better
Then
Better crawl and indexing efficiency
Then
Stronger technical authority
Result
Higher ranking potential

When two sites have similar authority, backlinks, and content, the one with stronger technical SEO tends to win. Clean technical signals give search engines the confidence to rank you higher, in classic search and in AI results alike.

Off-Page SEO / 01 · the link gap

Zero of the six link types that decide this market.

The existing links are generic guest posts, the kind any practice in any city could get. They carry no local signal and no industry trust. The links that separate a top local practice from the rest are the ones we are entirely missing.

Here is the part that matters most. Okanagan Dentistry has the exact same Authority Score as us, 10, and still holds page-1 visibility. The difference is not link volume. It is link category and relevance.

Off-Page SEO / 02 · the target list

The exact domains competitors have and we do not.

Pulled by comparing all three competitor link profiles against ours. Every domain below sits in a category Google associates with credibility in the dental space. These are the gaps to close, one category at a time.

Local / regional press

Off-Page SEO / 03 · quality over quantity

Some links we built are not even indexed.

Fourteen of the created backlinks do not appear to be indexed in Google right now. That does not make them harmful, but a link on a page Google never indexes delivers little lasting value. The strategy from here favours the right links over more links.

◐ PRIORITY 1Link insertion in aged content

Target pages already 6 to 12 months old, indexed, and topically relevant. Aged pages are already trusted. If a page survived that long without being dropped it is likely permanent, so this is the lowest-risk route: the trust is established, not being built.

✓ PRIORITY 2Guest posts on relevant domains

Not a marketing blog, not a finance site. Health or wellness adjacent at minimum, dental ideally. Each piece written to answer a real question first and place the link second.

The principle. Backlinks are a vote of confidence. For terms like dentist kelowna, a small number of trusted, relevant, permanently indexed links beats a large volume of generic or unstable ones. The goal is not more links. It is the right links, on pages Google already trusts, pointing to the pages that need to rank.

Off-Page SEO / what it earns

Where the link work leads.

Do
Aged content insertion · relevant guest posts · authority domains
Then
Consistent, trusted, topically relevant signals
Then
Domain and page-level trust increases
Then
A stronger backlink profile than competitors
Result
Higher ranking potential for the target keywords

Okanagan Dentistry proved it on this same map: identical authority to us, yet page-1, because their links come from the categories Google trusts. Closing that gap is how we win the close races at the top of the market.

On-Page SEO / 01 · over-optimisation

The homepage says "dentist in Kelowna" eleven times.

The exact phrase and its variations appear ten to eleven times on the homepage. Google ranks the page for relevance, but the copy reads mechanical, so patients skip it. This is very likely why positions exist and clicks do not follow.

＋ Zoom
Hero
＋ Zoom
Services
＋ Zoom
Dentists · FAQ
▲ The risk of leaving it

Pages at position 10 to 15 are technically ranking and practically invisible. No patient scrolls that far, and if they do, a keyword-heavy listing does not earn the click.

● The fix, with your sign-off

Reduce exact-match repetition from ten or eleven down to three to five, then rewrite for the patient: research top-ranking pages, read the audience stage, benchmark value propositions, and write with click psychology in mind.

REQUIRES CLIENT SIGN-OFF TO EDIT HOMEPAGE COPY
On-Page SEO / 02 · structure & AI readability

The homepage is hard for AI to read.

Two structural issues stop the homepage from being cleanly understood and quoted. The heading order is broken, and the content below the FAQ reads generic and repeats on the service pages.

▲ FINDING 1Heading order is broken
H1 Welcome To Your Dentist In Kelowna
H3 Request Your Consultation skips H2
H4 Trusted · Modern Office · Patients First
H2 Veneers · Implants · Whitening · Crowns
H3 FAQ, then boilerplate sections

Search engines and AI agents use the heading outline as the map of a page. When it jumps from H1 to H3 to H4 and the H2s appear later, out of order, there is no clean structure to extract. The page becomes hard to summarise and cite.

＋ Zoom
Live heading outline · discoversmiles.ca
▲ FINDING 2Content reads boilerplate

After the FAQ, the Our Mission, Why Choose Us and We Strive To sections read generic, and the same blocks appear on the service pages. Duplicated, templated copy gives Google and AI nothing unique to index or quote.

＋ Zoom
Homepage · generic sections below the FAQ
The fix. Restructure the headings into a clean H1 to H2 to H3 order so the page has a readable outline, then rewrite the generic sections into specific, patient-focused copy that is unique to each page and easy for AI to extract. This pairs with the homepage copy sign-off already noted.
● Sample page we have already built

We rebuilt one page as a working example: clean heading structure, unique patient-focused copy, and content an AI can lift and cite. This is just one page. We can do the same for every page on the site.

View the sample page ↗
Opportunities / 01 · fastest win

A better meta title turns impressions into clicks.

The current homepage title looks identical to every competitor in the results. There is no reason for a patient to choose Discover Dental over the four listings beside it. Rewriting it lifts click-through on rankings we already hold, with no change in position required.

Impressions already earned · 3 mo
0
The audience is already seeing us
Current click rate
0
The gap a stronger title closes directly
Ranking change needed
None
Pure CTR gain on existing visibility

This is the single fastest lever in the audit. Same rankings, more clicks, more booked patients, starting the day it ships.

Opportunities / 02 · the compounding move

Turn 40 scattered posts into three topic authorities.

Forty blog posts already rank for real patient questions and already link to the homepage. But the structure is flat: every blog points straight to the homepage with no topical hierarchy. Grouping them under three pillar pages tells Google and AI that this practice owns these subjects.

＋ Tap to zoom
Content hub · three pillars, real posts, one funnel to the homepage keywords
Pillar 1
Gum & Periodontal Health
Pillar 2
Restorative & Cosmetic Care
Pillar 3
Family & Emergency Care

Cluster to cluster, cluster to pillar, pillar to homepage. It reads to an AI engine like a book with chapters, which makes topical depth easy to understand and easy to cite. And it funnels that authority straight to the three keywords that pay.

The Whole Picture / connecting every lever

How the four levers move the three keywords.

Each section of this audit fixes one thing. Together they answer the opening problem: ranked, but not clicked. Here is how they compound into position.

TTechnical
Faster mobile, clean schema, fixed indexing, resubmitted sitemap.
Raises the ceiling
OOff-Page
Close the 0-of-6 link gap with the categories Google trusts.
Wins the tie
POn-Page
Cut mechanical repetition so the homepage reads human.
Earns the click
CContent
Stronger title now, pillar and cluster hub for the long game.
Compounds authority
TechnicalRemoves the friction that caps rank. Faster mobile and clean structure let Google and AI fully read and trust the site, and unlock the ten crawled-but-unindexed pages to rank for long-tail patient questions. Lifts the ceiling on all three keywords.
Off-PageBuilds the trust profile that decides close races. Okanagan outranks us on identical authority because of link category. Closing that gap moves the three commercial terms up page one.
On-PageConverts the rankings we already hold into clicks. A human homepage on the exact phrases already ranking turns impressions into visits.
ContentA stronger meta title lifts clicks on the 265,000 impressions we already get, with no ranking change. The pillar hub organises 40 posts into three authorities that Google and AI cite, funnelling depth to the homepage. Raises topical authority across the domain.

Impressions we have→ Position we lift→ Clicks we earn→ Patients we book
The Loop / how it all compounds

One loop: Google, AI, and patients.

The four fixes are not separate tasks. They feed one another. Every turn of this loop makes the site easier for Google to read, easier for AI to cite, and easier for patients to trust, which lifts the keywords and starts the turn again.

GOOGLE · crawls & ranks AI AGENTS · read & cite USERS · trust & click

Better structure earns rank. Rank earns clicks and bookings. Those signals reinforce trust with Google and AI, so the next turn is easier. That is how three keywords reach the Top 3 and stay there.

Thank you.

Every finding in this audit is backed by data you can open and check. When you are ready, we will prioritise the fixes and begin the climb to the Top 3.
